revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hazu-jinja (Nishio)]][[ja:幡頭神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Hazu Shrine | native_name = 幡頭神社 | image = Hazu-jinja (Nishio), haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hazu Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.778044|137.087542}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Hazu Shrine'''|幡頭神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mikawa Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11356190|ltq=Q11356190|qid=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} ** Series ordinal: 17 ** P155: {{ill|Kumaku Shrine|lt=Kumaku Shrine|ja|久麻久神社|qid=Q131932258|1=Kumaku Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039576|ltq=Q135039576|qid=Q135039576|1=Kataharano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q317844|ltq=Q317844|qid=Q317844|1=Nishio}} * {{ill|Mikawa Province|lt=Mikawa Province|ar|مقاطعة ميكاوا|ckb|پارێزگای میکاوا|cs|Provincie Mikawa|de|Provinz Mikawa|en|Mikawa Province|eo|Provinco Mikaŭa|es|Provincia de Mikawa|fa|ولایت میکاوا|fi|Mikawan provinssi|fr|Province de Mikawa|gl|Provincia de Mikawa|hak|Mikawa-koet|id|Provinsi Mikawa|it|Mikawa (provincia)|ja|三河国|ko|미카와국|lt|Mikavos žemė|nl|Mikawa (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Mikawa|pt|Província de Mikawa|ru|Микава (провинция)|simple|Mikawa Province|sk|Mikawa|sr|Провинција Микава|th|แคว้นมิกาวะ|tr|Mikawa (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Мікава|ur|میکاوا صوبہ|zh_classical|三河國|zh_min_nan|Mikawa-kok|zh_yue|三河國|zh|三河國|qid=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}} * {{ill|Q522936|ltq=Q522936|qid=Q522936|1=Hazu District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.778044|137.087542}} == image (P18) == * Hazu-jinja (Nishio), haiden.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181478 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Unranked|lt=Unranked|ja|无位|qid=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * Улас|ca|Japó|canews|Categoria:Japó|caquote|Japó|cbk_zam|Japón|cdo|Nĭk-buōng|ceb|Hapon|ce|Ипнойчоь|chr|ᏣᏩᏂᏏ|ch|Chapan|chy|Japan|ckb|ژاپۆن|co|Giappone|crh|Japon|csb|Japòńskô|cs|Japonsko|csnews|Kategorie:Japonsko|csquote|Japonsko|csvoyage|Japonsko|cu|Ꙗпѡнїꙗ|cv|Япони|cy|Japan|dag|Japan|da|Japan|de|Japan|denews|Kategorie:Japan|dequote|Japan|devoyage|Japan|din|Japan|diq|Japonya|dsb|Japańska|dtp|Jepun|dty|जापान|dv|ޖަޕާނު|dz|ཇ་པཱན|ee|Japan|el|Ιαπωνία|elnews|Κατηγορία:Ιαπωνία|elquote|Ιαπωνία|elvoyage|Ιαπωνία|eml|Giapòṅ|en|Japan|ennews|Category:Japan|enquote|Japan|envoyage|Japan|eo|Japanio|eonews|Kategorio:Japanujo|eoquote|Japanio|eovoyage|Japanio|es|Japón|esnews|Categoría:Japón|esquote|Japón|esvoyage|Japón|et|Jaapan|etquote|Jaapan|eu|Japonia|ext|Japón|fa|ژاپن|fanews|رده:ژاپن|faquote|ژاپن|favoyage|ژاپن|ff|Japan|fiu_vro|Jaapan|fi|Japani|finews|Luokka:Japani|fivoyage|Japani|fj|Japani|fo|Japan|frp|Japon|frr|Jaapan|fr|Japon|frnews|Catégorie:Japon|frquote|Japon|frvoyage|Japon|fur|Gjapon|fy|Japan|gag|Yaponiya|gan|日本|ga|An Мастор|my|ဂျပန်နိုင်ငံ|mzn|جاپون|nap|Giappone|nds_nl|Japan|nds|Japan|ne|जापान|new|जापान|nia|Jepang|nl|Japan|nlnews|Categorie:Japan|nlvoyage|Japan|nn|Japan|nov|Japan|no|Japan|nqo|ߗ߭ߊߔߐ߲߫|nrm|Japon|nr|Japane|nso|Japane|nv|Binááʼádaałtsʼózí Dineʼé Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * はずじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9180305007136 == Commons category (P373) == * Hazu-jinja (Nishio) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県西尾市吉良町宮崎前留谷60-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hazu-jinja (Nishio)}} [[JA:幡頭神社]] {{wikidata link|Q60988165}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Hazu Shrine |Image = [[File:Hazu shrine1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nishio City|Q317844|qid=Q317844|1=Nishio}} 60-1 Maedomeya, Miyazaki, Kira-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|46|41.2|N|137|5|14.9|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Hazu Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Takeinadane no Mikoto|Q11486691|qid=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founded = (traditional) {{ill|lt=Taihō era|Q1346269|qid=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=702|Q23497|qid=Q23497|1=702}}) |Main hall = Three-bay {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style, cypress bark roof |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = First Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi#Japan|label=Hazu Shrine|lat_deg=34|lat_min=46|lat_sec=41.2|lon_deg=137|lon_min=5|lon_sec=14.9|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|46|41.2|N|137|05|14.9|E}}}} }} [[File:Hazu shrine3.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Hazu Shrine''' (はずじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Miyazaki, Kira-chō, {{ill|lt=Nishio City|Q317844|qid=Q317844|1=Nishio}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Takeinadane no Mikoto|Q11486691|qid=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} The principal deity is {{ill|lt=Takeinadane no Mikoto|Q11486691|qid=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}}, who is said to have been the {{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko of Owari|Q11465294|qid=Q11465294|1=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko}} and, according to tradition, served as “Hatagashira” (banner commander) during {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}’s eastern expedition. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} are also enshrined together. === History === It is traditionally said that the shrine buildings were established in Taihō 2 (702). According to legend, the enshrined deity Takeinadane no Mikoto died at sea off Izu while returning from the eastern campaign with Prince Yamato Takeru, and his body drifted ashore at the coast of Miyazaki, where local villagers enshrined him[https://www.japan-net.ne.jp/~kira-spa/sightseeing_spot/01-10.html Hazu Shrine - Mikawa Bay Quasi-National Park, Kira Miyazaki Coast, Kira Onsen] Retrieved December 5, 2018. In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō era|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 (927), the shrine appears as “Hazu Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Mikawa Province|Q768158|qid=Q768158|lt=Mikawa|1=Mikawa Province}} {{ill|lt=Hazu District|Q522936|qid=Q522936|1=Hazu District}}, and is listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Hazu Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. It is also recorded in the *Mikawa Province Register of Deities* as “Senior First Rank Haridai Myōjin, seated in Hazu District”{{Sfn|Hazu Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}. The present main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenshō era|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 8 (1580). In {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 4 (1871), under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it became a village shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1921|Q2162|qid=Q2162|1=1921}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō era|Q329174|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 10) it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. On April 30, 1921 (Taishō 10), the main hall was designated a Specially Protected Building under the then {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Ancient Shrines and Temples|Q11411552|qid=Q11411552|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law}}Ministry of Education Notification No. 352, April 30, Taishō 10 (see: [{{NDLDC|2954737}} National Diet Library Digital Collections]). After the enforcement of the {{ill|lt=Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Q1188502|qid=Q1188502|1=Cultural Properties Protection Law}} on August 29, {{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|qid=Q18597|1=1950}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa era|Q327567|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 25), it became designated as an {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. In {{ill|lt=1957|Q5311|qid=Q5311|1=1957}} (Shōwa 32), the main halls of the subordinate Shinmei Shrine and Kumano Shrine were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture. From September 1, {{ill|lt=2015|Q2002|qid=Q2002|1=2015}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei era|Q234510|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 27) to September 30, {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|qid=Q25245|1=2016}} (Heisei 28), preservation and restoration work on the main hall was carried out under the leadership of the Nishio City Board of Education{{ill|lt=#Report on the Restoration of the Important Cultural Property Hazu Shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=“Report on the Preservation and Restoration Work of the Important Cultural Property Hazu Shrine Main Hall” 2018.}} p. 69.. In {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|qid=Q49625|1=2022}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa era|Q62609017|qid=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa|1=Reiwa era}} 4), the main halls of the subordinate Shinmei Shrine and Kumano Shrine were additionally designated as National Important Cultural Properties. === Grounds === [[File:Hazu shrine2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (National {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}})}}{{small|Kumano Shrine and Shinmei Shrine on the left and right.}}]] The shrine grounds are situated on a hill overlooking {{ill|lt=Mikawa Bay|Q1154346|qid=Q1154346|1=Mikawa Bay}}. The three-bay {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} main hall with a cypress bark roof dates from Tenshō 8 (1580). Though small in scale, its decorative motifs employ robust curved lines, and the interior carvings and *kamaboko* (strut) forms are simple in style. The roof is covered with cypress bark, and the deeply curved eaves are a distinctive feature[https://www.city.nishio.aichi.jp/index.cfm/8,14703,91,408,html Cultural Properties of Nishio City: Hazu Shrine Main Hall - Nishio City] Retrieved December 5, 2018. In addition to the main hall, the grounds include a worship hall, side hall, sacred horse shelter, purification pavilion, and shrine office. There are also subordinate shrines: Shinmei Shrine and Kumano Shrine. File:Hazu-jinja (Nishio), Shinmei-sha.jpg|Shinmei Shrine (National Important Cultural Property) File:Hazu-jinja (Nishio), Kumano-sha.jpg|Kumano Shrine (National Important Cultural Property) === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties (National designation) === * Hazu Shrine, 3 structures (buildings) – The main hall was designated on April 30, Taishō 10 (1921){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|1278|Hazu Shrine Main Hall}}, and on September 20, Reiwa 4 (2022), the main halls of the subordinate Kumano Shrine and Shinmei Shrine were additionally designatedMinistry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 121, September 20, Reiwa 4.. ** Main hall (with 3 attached ridge tags) – constructed in Tenshō 8 (1580). ** Subordinate Kumano Shrine main hall (with 4 attached ridge tags) – constructed in Tenshō 8 (1580). ** Subordinate Shinmei Shrine main hall (with 4 attached ridge tags) – constructed in Tenshō 8 (1580). The subordinate Kumano Shrine main hall and Shinmei Shrine main hall had previously been designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture in Shōwa 32 (1957). === Local information === '''Address''' * 60-1 Maedomeya, Miyazaki, {{ill|lt=Kira-chō|Q1154383|qid=Q1154383|1=Kira, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Nishio City|Q317844|qid=Q317844|1=Nishio}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu|Q30850|qid=Q30850|1=Meitetsu}} (Nagoya Railroad) {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nishio Line|Q6810417|qid=Q6810417|1=Meitetsu Nishio Line}} or {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Gamagōri Line|Q6810403|qid=Q6810403|1=Meitetsu Gamagōri Line}} {{ill|lt=Kira-Yoshida Station|Q6414740|qid=Q6414740|1=Kira Yoshida Station}}, approximately 7 minutes by taxi (car) === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Hazu Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|Hazu Shrine (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|editor=Association for the
